title: cronsplain explain: lib/explain.js + tests/test_explain.js (parsed cron -> deterministic English)
description: The `explain` renderer: turns a cron expression into one deterministic human-readable English sentence. Depends only on T-045 (parser) + T-044 (fields display names) — it has NO dependency on the schedule track, so it runs in parallel with the tournament (independent frontier of wave 3).
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY: projects/cronsplain/lib/explain.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_explain.js. Touch no other file. require()s ../lib/parser (parse) and ../lib/fields (MONTH_DISPLAY, DOW_DISPLAY).

EXPORT explain(expr) -> string: parse via parser.parse, then render a single deterministic English sentence. (You MAY also export explainParsed(parsed) and have explain compose parse+explainParsed — document which.) Same expr ALWAYS yields the same string (tests depend on it).

RENDER each field readably: '*' -> 'every minute'/'every hour'/etc.; step '*/n' -> 'every N minutes/hours/...'; list/range -> readable phrasing ('minutes 0 and 30', 'hours 9 through 17'); month/day-of-week numbers -> names via fields display arrays ('January', 'Monday'). When BOTH dayOfMonth and dayOfWeek are restricted, the prose MUST express the OR coupling (e.g. 'on the 13th of the month OR on Friday'). Pick a clear sentence template and keep it stable.
ERRORS: an invalid expr should surface cleanly — either let CronParseError propagate (documented) or wrap it; do NOT emit a stack trace from explain itself. Document the choice.

tests/test_explain.js (node:test + node:assert): assert the EXACT sentence for a handful of exprs including '*/15 * * * *', '0 0 * * *', '0 9-17 * * 1-5', and a both-restricted OR case like '0 0 13 * 5'; assert an invalid expr throws/surfaces CronParseError.
SHARED CONVENTIONS: CommonJS, zero deps, deterministic output. Run from projects/cronsplain/.
HARNESS PROTOCOL: claim/lock(lib/explain.js, tests/test_explain.js)/in_progress/handoff --to-role verifier; NEVER self-mark done; --agent <you>.

VERIFY (in handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/ `node --test tests/test_explain.js` green; `node -e "console.log(require('./lib/explain').explain('*/15 * * * *'))"` prints your deterministic sentence.

notes.note: plan: implement lib/explain.js rendering each cron field (wildcard/step/range/list) into English via fields.MONTH_DISPLAY/DOW_DISPLAY, DOM-OR-DOW coupling when both restricted, propagate CronParseError from parser.parse; write tests/test_explain.js with node:test asserting exact sentences plus invalid-expr throw.

HANDOFF -> verifier: WORKER DONE, replay to verify. Files owned/touched: projects/cronsplain/lib/explain.js (new), projects/cronsplain/tests/test_explain.js (new). No other file touched.
REPLAY: cd projects/cronsplain then run: node --test tests/test_explain.js  -> EXPECTED/OBSERVED: tests 19, pass 19, fail 0. Also ran full suite node --test tests/*.js -> tests 57, pass 57, fail 0 (no regression vs T-044/T-045 tests).
VERIFY command from acceptance criteria: node -e "console.log(require(./lib/explain).explain(*/15 * * * *))" (fill in real quotes when replaying) -> OBSERVED stdout: Every 15 minutes, every day.
Sample outputs (expr -> explain(expr)), all asserted exactly in tests/test_explain.js:
* * * * *  ->  Every minute.
*/15 * * * *  ->  Every 15 minutes, every day.
0 0 * * *  ->  At 00:00, every day.
0 9-17 * * 1-5  ->  At minute 0 past every hour from 9 through 17, on Monday through Friday.
0 0 13 * 5 (OR)  ->  At 00:00, on the 13th of the month or on Friday.
*/15 0 * * *  ->  Every 15 minutes during hour 0, every day.
0 9 * * 1-5  ->  At 09:00, on Monday through Friday.
30 9 1 * *  ->  At 09:30, on the 1st of the month.
0 0 1 * 1 (OR)  ->  At 00:00, on the 1st of the month or on Monday.
0,30 * * * *  ->  At minutes 0 and 30 past every hour, every day.
0 0 1 1,6 *  ->  At 00:00, on the 1st of the month, in January and June.
Invalid: explain(@daily) throws CronParseError (expression=@daily); explain(60 0 * * *) throws CronParseError with field=minute.
DESIGN/DEVIATION notes for review:
1. explain(expr) = explainParsed(parse(expr)); explainParsed(parsed) also exported for pre-parsed input, per the OPTIONAL clause in the acceptance criteria.
2. Error handling choice: CronParseError propagates UNCHANGED, undocumented-wrapping rejected by design (documented in the module docblock) -- matches criteria bullet 4 wording "either...or", chose propagate.
3. Field-shape classification (every/single/range/step/rangeStep/list) is inferred from the Set numeric shape, not raw cron text (parser does not retain per-field raw text except the two *Restricted booleans) -- documented at top of lib/explain.js. This means e.g. */15 and the equivalent explicit list 0,15,30,45 render identically (intentional, same schedule).
4. A 2-point uniform-diff Set (e.g. from */30) renders as an explicit list (minutes 0 and 30) rather than every 30 minutes -- this reproduces the delegators own pinned example wording exactly and avoids overclaiming step semantics from only 2 data points.
5. OR-coupling wording uses lowercase or (on X or on Y) rather than AND, since the coupling is semantically a union/OR per parser.js docblock and the acceptance criteria own example itself says OR.
6. Sentence omits a month clause entirely when month is unrestricted (no filler every month); when literally every field is unrestricted the whole sentence collapses to the fixed Every minute. per the pinned acceptance example (no redundant every day suffix in that one case only).
Acceptance-criteria checklist:
- OWNS EXACTLY explain.js + test_explain.js, no other file touched: YES (git status confirms only these two new files; T-045.json/T-046.json diffs in repo are from a concurrent T-046 worker, not this task).
- EXPORT explain(expr)->string via parser.parse, deterministic: YES, plus explainParsed documented.
- Field rendering (wildcard/step/list/range, month+dow names via MONTH_DISPLAY/DOW_DISPLAY): YES, covered by dedicated tests.
- Both-restricted OR coupling expressed in prose: YES (0 0 13 * 5 and 0 0 1 * 1 cases).
- Errors: CronParseError propagates cleanly, no stack trace from explain.js itself: YES, documented + tested.
- tests/test_explain.js covers required exprs (*/15 * * * *, 0 0 * * *, 0 9-17 * * 1-5, both-restricted OR, invalid throw): YES, all present plus extra branch coverage (19 tests total).
- CommonJS, zero deps, deterministic, run from projects/cronsplain: YES.
No blockers, no deviations requiring a decision from the verifier beyond reviewing the sentence-wording choices above.

VERIFIED (verifier-b, independent replay + cognitive-diversity). REPLAY: from projects/cronsplain 'node --test tests/*.js' -> tests 57 pass 57 fail 0; 'node --test tests/test_explain.js' -> tests 19 pass 19 fail 0. VERIFY cmd node -e explain('*/15 * * * *') -> 'Every 15 minutes, every day.' (exact). OR-COUPLING (load-bearing union, not intersection): '0 0 13 * 5' -> 'At 00:00, on the 13th of the month or on Friday.'; '0 0 1 * 1' -> 'At 00:00, on the 1st of the month or on Monday.' Both dom+dow restricted (dom={13}/{1}, dow={5}/{1}) render with 'or' matching parser.js documented Vixie union truth. PARSER-CONSISTENCY (no-lie): dow-only-restricted '0 0 * * 3'->'on Wednesday', '0 0 * * 1-5'->'on Monday through Friday' -- neither says 'every day' (saysEveryDay=false). Extra probes not in suite: '0 0 * * 0'->'on Sunday', '0 0 * * 6'->'on Saturday', '0 0 1 1 *'->'in January', '*/30'->'minutes 0 and 30' (2-pt list, disclosed/acceptable). ERROR-PROP: explain('@daily') & explain('not a cron') throw CronParseError (field=undefined, whole-expr); explain('60 0 * * *') field=minute; explain('0 0 * * 8') field=dayOfWeek -- all unchanged, no wrap, no stack-trace-from-explain. FROZEN T-044: MONTH_DISPLAY/DOW_DISPLAY still frozen=true and byte-identical after many explain calls (read-only). Zero deps, CommonJS, exports {explain,explainParsed}. DISCIPLINE: only 2 owned new files (lib/explain.js, tests/test_explain.js); candidates/ + T-045/T-046 json diffs are concurrent schedule-track work, not this task; no active locks (handoff clean). P-019 RULING: substrate-worker-3 registration (by=main, 05:07:02Z) is CONSISTENT/AUTHORIZED -- session.py gates mechanically on AMBIENT identity in coordinator set {main, fable-5-coordinator} (fail-closed), NOT on --by; a fail-closed gate means the record's existence proves ambient was a coordinator; ambient='main' is exactly the legitimate in-session coordinator pattern P-019 preserves. NOT a violation.
